channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: Anubis ransomware claims Coca-Cola Fairlife attack, threatens data leak
item.description: The Anubis ransomware gang has claimed responsibility for the cyberattack on Coca-Cola's Fairlife dairy subsidiary, threatening to publish allegedly stolen corporate data unless the company pays a ran...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hacker Turns AI Jailbreaks Into Offensive Attack Platform
item.description: A Russian-speaking actor, "Trim," dismantled publicly available frontier models and integrated them with offensive security tools....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: 380 investors face Bitcoin mining losses after alleged $22M US scheme put just 13% into mining
item.description: More than 380 investors supplied the funds, while court approval, penalties and any recovery remain unresolved. The post 380 investors face Bitcoin mining losses after alleged $22M US scheme put just ...
item.dc:creator: CryptoSlate

item.title: Cardano’s NIGHT Hits All-Time Low After 290M Token Dump
item.description: On-chain analysis showed no increase in NIGHT's total supply, suggesting the selloff was linked to existing tokens rather than new issuance....
item.dc:creator: CryptoPotato

item.description: Without revealing any details about the actual agreement from President Trump on potential presidential restrictions, his White House is urging its acceptance....

item.description: The filing comes after months of upheaval that included a controversial market-making agreement, an internal investigation into its MOVE token launch and a Binance ban tied to its market maker....

item.description: President Donald Trump has reportedly agreed to proposed ethics restrictions tied to sweeping US cryptocurrency legislation, addressing one of the biggest obstacles in months of negotiations and shift...

item.title: Bitcoin Price Analysis: Is $70K Next After BTC Broke Above $66K?

item.title: Critical wp2shell WordPress flaws exploited to install webshells
item.description: Hackers are exploiting the "wp2shell" critical vulnerability suite (CVE-2026-63030 and CVE-2026-60137) affecting WordPress Core to deploy persistent webshells and install malicious plugins on affected...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: The $1.2 billion options wall came down, and this time Bitcoin actually moved
item.description: For most of the month, traders had a pretty good explanation for Bitcoin's refusal to budge. A dense cluster of options contracts, they argued, was holding the price in a cage between $60,000 and $65,...
item.dc:creator: CryptoSlate

item.dc:creator: Coindesk

item.description: The crypto financial services firm is funding developers to strengthen the blockchain's security before quantum computers become powerful enough to steal billions in crypto....

item.title: AWS Kiro Flaw Let a Poisoned Web Page Rewrite Its Config and Run Code
item.description: Hidden text on a web page was enough to make Kiro, AWS's agentic coding IDE, rewrite its own configuration file and run an attacker's code on a developer's machine, with no approval step able to stop ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Critical SharePoint RCE CVE-2026-50522 Under Active Exploitation After Public PoC
item.description:
A third SharePoint Server flaw patched by Microsoft as part of its Patch Tuesday update for July 2026 has come under active exploitation, per watchTowr.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-5052...

item.title: Fewer Than 300 Made the Cut: Ivan Nevzorov on MiCA, CASP Licensing, and What’s Next for Crypto Firms
item.description: Of the more than 3,000 companies that were legally serving crypto clients across the EU as recently as this June, fewer than 300 hold the right to do so today, according to CASP Tracker. The reason is...
item.dc:creator: CryptoPotato

item.title: Qilin Ransomware Attackers Exploit PAN-OS Authentication Bypass for Initial Access
item.description:
Threat actors have been observed exploiting a now-patched high-severity Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS vulnerability as an entry point to deploy Qilin (aka Agenda) ransomware on victim environments.
Arcti...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New 7-Zip Vulnerability Could Let Crafted XZ Archives Run Code During Extraction
item.description: Opening a crafted XZ archive in 7-Zip could let an attacker run code on the machine. The flaw, CVE-2026-14266, is a heap-based buffer overflow in how the archiver processes XZ chunked data, and Trend ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Russian-Speaking Hacker Uses Google Gemini CLI to Control Botnet of Eight Dental Clinic PCs
item.description:
A solo Russian-speaking threat actor known as "bandcampro" outsourced a chunk of their operations to Google's open-source Gemini CLI artificial intelligence (AI) and commandeered a live botnet.
The f...

item.title: HOLLOWGRAPH: Turning Microsoft 365 Calendars into Covert Command-and-Control Channels
item.description: Group-IB uncovers HOLLOWGRAPH, a Windows malware that abuses Microsoft Graph API to exfiltrate files and receive commands from the attacker using Microsoft 365 calendar events, and DNS tunneling to re...

item.title: World's Largest AI Model Repository Hugging Face Breached by Autonomous AI Agent
item.description:
In an ironic twist, open-source artificial intelligence (AI) platform Hugging Face revealed that it was the victim of a hack perpetrated by an autonomous AI agent system.
The company said it detected...

item.title: Critical NGINX Vulnerability Can Crash Workers and May Allow Remote Code Execution
item.description: F5 has shipped fixes for a critical nginx flaw that lets a remote, unauthenticated attacker trigger a heap buffer overflow in the worker process with crafted HTTP requests. CVE-2026-42533 was patched ...
